summary: Reviewed AmbientEditorialHero.tsx, BrandMark.tsx, globals.css, hero concepts 29–31, and logo options 151–155. Hero concept 31 is the strongest direction (bilateral symmetry, single accent spine, organ arches). Logo option 153 is the strongest mark (paired ECG-curve silhouette, teal/terra split, serif tagline). Current code has 6 specific premium-killing issues that can be fixed in under 2 hours.
recommendations: {"id":"R1","priority":1,"title":"Adopt concept-31 globe anatomy as canonical and cut the annotation noise","rationale":"Concept 31 is the only hero that reads as deliberate architectural illustration rather than a scaled clipart figure. The bilateral torso silhouette + single vertical accent spine is what Apple or Anthropic would ship. Concepts 29 and 30 both scatter attention. The current AmbientEditorialHero SVG (lines 120–125) has 3 annotation circles + 3 note-line paths that add zero information at hero scale.","exact_cuts":[{"file":"AmbientEditorialHero.tsx","lines":"120-125","action":"Delete all six elements: the three \u003ccircle className=\u0027hero-anatomy-annotation\u0027/\u003e and three \u003cpath className=\u0027hero-anatomy-note\u0027/\u003e elements entirely. They render as decorative dots with stems — noise, not signal."},{"file":"AmbientEditorialHero.tsx","line":"80","action":"Reduce pan range from ±34/±22 to ±12/±8: change the two multiplier literals on line 80 from `34` → `12` and `22` → `8`. Current range is aggressive and feels cheap on a medical product."}]}

{"id":"R2","priority":2,"title":"Kill the hero-proof-line hardcoded copy and fix the semantic \u003ci /\u003e divider","rationale":"Line 150 has hardcoded small text \u0027Original questions, cleaner rationale, and direct package paths.\u0027 that duplicates the body prop and reads like a dev placeholder. The `\u003ci /\u003e` on line 149 is semantically wrong (italic element used as a visual divider).","exact_cuts":[{"file":"AmbientEditorialHero.tsx","lines":"147-151","action":"Remove the entire \u003cdiv className=\u0027hero-proof-line\u0027\u003e block. The eyebrow, body, and stats row already carry this signal. If a divider line is needed below the CTA, replace with \u003chr aria-hidden=\u0027true\u0027 className=\u0027hero-rule\u0027 /\u003e styled in CSS."}]}

{"id":"R3","priority":3,"title":"Adopt logo option 153\u0027s ECG-curve concept into BrandMark.tsx — drop the current three-arch collapse","rationale":"Option 153 (two organic ECG-style curves + horizontal rule + wordmark) is the only mark in the 151–155 batch that reads as medically distinct AND survives stripping the card container. The paired curves suggest both pulse trace and body silhouette — dual read, one stroke. The current BrandMark glyph (axis + circle + three arches) collapses to noise at 44px nav size because the three arches stack too closely. Options 152/154 are generic SaaS geometry. Option 151 is a Figma placeholder. Option 155 has a strong eye-mark but \u0027PREMIUM NURSING PREP\u0027 is self-congratulatory and must never appear in the mark.","exact_cuts":[{"file":"BrandMark.tsx","lines":"11-27","action":"Replace the current 5-element SVG glyph (axis, circle, arch, basin, ring) with two curves matching option-153 geometry: a primary dark curve (stroke rgba(31,38,43,0.82) sw:2.1) and a secondary teal curve offset 18px lower (stroke rgba(90,127,136,0.74) sw:1.7). Remove the circle/top element entirely — it adds top-heaviness at small sizes."},{"file":"globals.css","lines":"147-170","action":"Remove .site-mark-glyph-axis, .site-mark-glyph-top, .site-mark-glyph-arch, .site-mark-glyph-basin, .site-mark-glyph-ring class rules. Replace with two classes: .site-mark-glyph-primary (dark curve) and .site-mark-glyph-secondary (teal curve)."}]}

{"id":"R4","priority":4,"title":"Tighten globals.css color system — three stroke opacities on the same mark destroy premium perception","rationale":"The current BrandMark glyph uses four different opacity levels across five strokes (0.88 terra axis, 0.62 terra circle, 0.42 teal arch, 0.58 terra basin, 0.26 teal ring). Every logo option 151–155 repeats this mistake of mixing 3+ stroke values in one mark. Apple, Anthropic, and ChatGPT use exactly one stroke color per mark, sometimes two with a strong hierarchy. The fix is: primary stroke = rgba(31,38,43,0.88), accent stroke = rgba(90,127,136,0.72). Terra (#C29A56) belongs only in the axis/spine element of the hero globe — not in the nav mark.","exact_cuts":[{"file":"globals.css","lines":"148-170","action":"Consolidate to two stroke rules inside the mark: primary dark at opacity 0.88, teal accent at opacity 0.72. Remove all terra/gold strokes from the nav mark. Terra is a hero-only accent."}]}

durable_memory: Hero concept 31 is the canonical direction: bilateral symmetry torso, single accent spine, teal/terra organ arches, no annotation dots
Logo option 153 (ECG dual-curve + horizontal rule) is the chosen mark direction — survives container removal, dual medical read
Current BrandMark.tsx three-arch glyph collapses at 44px; scheduled for replacement with two-curve 153 geometry
Terra (#C29A56) is hero-only accent — must not appear in nav mark strokes
Mouse parallax limits: max ±12px X / ±8px Y for medical/premium positioning
active_context: AmbientEditorialHero.tsx has 6 annotation elements (lines 120–125) pending deletion
BrandMark.tsx glyph replacement blocked pending human sign-off on option-153 curve geometry
globals.css mark stroke rules need consolidation to 2 values

current_blocker: Option 153 curve geometry in the SVG concept card uses a card-container wash that does visual work the mark itself cannot do alone. The actual curve paths need to be stress-tested at 44px (nav), 24px (favicon), and dark-background contexts before committing BrandMark.tsx replacement. This requires a human to render the raw mark paths without the card and approve.
suggested_human_fix: Extract the two curve paths from chapai-option-153.svg (lines 11–12: the dark curve and teal curve), render them at 44×44 on both #F4F2EA and #1F262B backgrounds, screenshot both, and confirm the dual-read (ECG trace + body silhouette) survives. If yes, give the go-ahead and the BrandMark.tsx swap is a 20-minute cut. If either curve needs tightening, adjust the control points before committing.
next_task: Implement R1 + R2 (annotation cut + proof-line removal + parallax reduction) in AmbientEditorialHero.tsx — these are zero-risk, no design approval required, and immediately lift perceived quality. Ship those first while the logo direction is confirmed by human review.
